reasoning: The FedRAMP CM-2 (2) control focuses on maintaining the accuracy and currency of baseline configurations through automation. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that nodes and control plane components (like the API server) are automatically upgraded to maintain security and stability. Additionally, admission controllers can be used to enforce automatic patch management policies for workloads. Backup configurations ensure that the cluster state is preserved and can be restored, aligning with maintaining configuration currency.
title: Baseline Configuration | Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
